specific_location: Greenhorn Creek 1/4 mile upstream from Rye Trout Farm
description: Bird was a small kinglet sized wren with a short stubby tail <1/2 body length, and held upright almost perpendicular to plump body. Dark horizontal bars crossed the tail.  Upperparts were dark rufous brown with little to no mottling nor speckles on crown and back. Bird had a buffy supercilium. Bill was thin and dark. Wings were rufous brown with dark vertical bars.  Underparts were a bright uniform rich buff including throat which did not contrast at all with buffy breast and upper belly, making distinction from similar Winter Wrens fairly easy. Barring was confined to extreme lower belly and undertail coverts. Legs were a dull flesh color.Behaviors: Foraged among stick piles along Greenhorn Creek upstream from Rye Trout Farm. Occasionally perched on stick piles giving good looks.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
